ideasAccepted.idea: Compact SKILL.md with optional references directory for deeper detail
ideasAccepted.reason: Keeps router small and moves bulk to Haus-owned reference files.

ideasAccepted.idea: Explicit use when and do not use when sections on each skill surface
ideasAccepted.reason: Supports bounded recommender selection and clear handoff to agents.

ideasAccepted.idea: YAML frontmatter keys name and description on each skill file
ideasAccepted.reason: Stable metadata for authors and tooling without new runtime paths.

ideasRejected.idea: Ship verbatim upstream example bodies as ready-to-use Haus skills
ideasRejected.reason: Violates Haus policy: ship rewritten guidance only; verbatim copy risks drift and attribution noise.

ideasRejected.idea: Treat upstream examples as canonical catalog entries without review
ideasRejected.reason: Would bypass curation gates and stack fit checks for this product.

ideasAccepted.idea: Red-green-refactor habit for risky edits documented as a curation note only
ideasAccepted.reason: Captures test-first discipline in Haus voice without stack file edits or new hooks.

ideasAccepted.idea: Pause for one clarifying question when task scope is unclear before broad scans
ideasAccepted.reason: Reduces wasted context and matches bounded Haus workflow tone.

ideasAccepted.idea: Close work with intent, files touched, and validation stated as evidence before claims
ideasAccepted.reason: Aligns contributors with evidence-style review already used in Haus agents.

ideasRejected.idea: Multi-agent parallel handoffs as a required default workflow
ideasRejected.reason: Conflicts with Haus stance on bounded single-session work and explicit review agents.

ideasRejected.idea: Long scripted session templates copied from upstream into shipping assets
ideasRejected.reason: High maintenance overlap with Haus routers; verbatim template copy is out of scope.

ideasAccepted.idea: Progressive disclosure: load deep detail only when the task touches that surface
ideasAccepted.reason: Documentation-only habit; no ECC coupling and no change to token scoring.

ideasAccepted.idea: Thin router SKILL versus deeper reference files for the same topic
ideasAccepted.reason: Reinforces Haus router shape using ECC-style framing as inspiration only.

ideasRejected.idea: Ship ECC-specific formats or treat ECC as a runtime dependency
ideasRejected.reason: Haus stays decoupled from external index formats; candidate sources remain inspiration-only.

ideasRejected.idea: Embed numeric token tables from any external index into recommender code
ideasRejected.reason: Token and scoring behavior is out of scope for curation-only PRs.

ideasAccepted.idea: Public discovery index is input for search only not catalog truth
ideasAccepted.reason: Prevents treating listings as curated manifest rows without Haus review.

ideasRejected.idea: Auto-import index entries into library catalog manifest
ideasRejected.reason: Would add unsupervised catalog growth and bypass manual curation review.

ideasRejected.idea: Trust listing quality or license fields without Haus verification
ideasRejected.reason: Discovery feeds vary; catalog policy requires explicit accept or reject per item.

ideasAccepted.idea: Stable machine ids haus dot prefix and stack slugs aligned to allowed-stacks policy
ideasAccepted.reason: Matches existing manifest conventions and keeps catalog audits predictable.

ideasRejected.idea: Wire external registry resolution into install or apply paths
ideasRejected.reason: Would introduce new integration surfaces beyond rewritten Haus-owned assets.

ideasRejected.idea: Paste upstream package README prose into Haus reference files
ideasRejected.reason: Verbatim external prose is disallowed; paraphrase into Haus-owned bullets if needed.

ideasAccepted.idea: Co-locate references directory beside SKILL.md for each skill folder
ideasAccepted.reason: Matches Haus layout and helps authors find depth next to the router entry.

ideasRejected.idea: Import upstream skill trees wholesale into plugin or library folders
ideasRejected.reason: Would bypass rewrite policy; maintain Haus-owned text and explicit targets only.

ideasRejected.idea: Depend on a personal or org brand namespace as a product requirement
ideasRejected.reason: Haus catalog stays neutral; names follow haus dot ids and internal conventions.

ideasAccepted.idea: Machine-readable schema for source decisions plus yarn sources decisions CI gate
ideasAccepted.reason: Documents existing validation artifact and keeps audits reproducible without new runtime.

ideasAccepted.idea: Explicit validate-before-merge step using source-decisions schema in the curation loop
ideasAccepted.reason: Ties contributor workflow to the shipped validator already wired in package scripts.

ideasRejected.idea: Treat external packaging marketplace as install source of truth
ideasRejected.reason: Haus ships curated library content only; no registry-driven install path.

ideasRejected.idea: Copy external validation UI copy into Haus docs without rewrite
ideasRejected.reason: Same as skills policy: inspiration only; wording must be Haus-owned.

ideasRejected.idea: Use VoltAgent primitives or architecture patterns in Haus
ideasRejected.reason: Python-first framework. Python is an unsupported stack in Haus. No ideas extracted.

ideasAccepted.idea: Maintain a community-facing index as a discovery signal during source auditing
ideasAccepted.reason: Validates that public discovery indices (everything.cc, skills.sh, prpm) are useful audit inputs, not catalog truth.

ideasRejected.idea: Import everything.cc listings directly into Haus catalog without per-item audit
ideasRejected.reason: Bypasses curation gates. Index entries must be individually audited before any can install.

ideasAccepted.idea: Record official Anthropic doc URLs as reference-only entries in curation-decisions.json
ideasAccepted.reason: Provides stable, authoritative URL references for skill authors without copying doc content.

ideasRejected.idea: Copy official Anthropic documentation prose verbatim into Haus skills or agents
ideasRejected.reason: No reuse license on documentation content. All Haus text must be Haus-owned.
